question_text: During winter solstice in the UK, the Sun is at its lowest angle in the sky. Which of the following best explains why the Sun's energy is spread over a larger area of ground surface in winter compared to summer?

options.label: A
options.text: The Sun is further away from Earth in winter, so its rays spread out more

options.label: B
options.text: The low angle of the Sun means its rays hit the ground at a shallow angle, spreading energy over a larger surface area

options.label: C
options.text: The Earth's atmosphere is thicker in winter, scattering the Sun's rays over a wider area

options.label: D
options.text: The rotation speed of Earth is slower in winter, allowing rays to spread further

explanation: When the Sun is at a low angle (winter), parallel rays of sunlight hit the ground at an oblique angle, causing the same amount of energy to be distributed over a larger surface area compared to when the Sun is high in the sky (summer). This is why winter receives less concentrated energy per unit area.
